title: Felicia australis
taxonomy: Felicia australis is a member of the daisy family, Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Felicia. The species is native to Australia and is often classified as a flowering plant. The classification of Felicia australis also includes it as a succulent species. It is further classified under the family classification as a part of the infraspecific taxonomic rank.
uses: Felicia australis, also known as the Blue Felicia or Blue Eyed Daisy, has been traditionally used in African and European folk medicine. The plant's leaves and flowers are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. In traditional medicine, the plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The leaves and flowers are often made into teas, infusions, or applied topically to the affected areas. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Felicia australis for medicinal purposes.
